info.title: Moqui Tools REST API
info.description: Services for various system administration and other tools in Moqui Framework. See more information             about Moqui Framework at [http://www.moqui.org/framework](http://www.moqui.org/framework/index.html)

tags.name: dataDocuments
tags.description: Data Documents - Database derived JSON documents, feeds

tags.name: basic
tags.description: Basic Entities - Enum, Geo, Status, UOM, etc

tags.name: systemMessages
tags.description: System to System Messages - Queue, Send, Receive, Consume, etc

parameters.name: pageIndex
parameters.description: Page number to return, starting with zero

parameters.name: pageSize
parameters.description: Number of records per page (default 100)

parameters.name: orderByField
parameters.description: Field name to order by (or comma separated names)

parameters.name: pageNoLimit
parameters.description: If true don't limit page size (no pagination)

parameters.name: dependentLevels
parameters.description: Levels of dependent child records to include

parameters.name: totalSquaredTime
parameters.description:
Total (sum) of the squared running times for
            calculating incremental standard deviation.

parameters.name: slowHitCount
parameters.description:
After 100 hits count of hits more that 2.6
            standard deviations above average (both avg and std dev adjusted incrementally).

parameters.name: pageIndex
parameters.description: Page number to return, starting with zero

parameters.name: pageSize
parameters.description: Number of records per page (default 100)

parameters.name: orderByField
parameters.description: Field name to order by (or comma separated names)

parameters.name: pageNoLimit

parameters.name: dependentLevels

parameters.name: dataDocumentId
parameters.description:
For ElasticSearch compatibility, and as a general good
            practice for use as a dynamic view-entity, must follow entity name convention of camel case and starting with a capital letter.

parameters.name: documentName
parameters.description:
The name of the document for display in search
            results and such. Is generally expanded on display and may use any field in the DataDocument.

parameters.name: documentTitle
parameters.description:
A title for each document instance for display in
            search results or other places. Meant to be string expanded using a "flattened" version of the document
            (see the CollectionUtilities.flattenNestedMap() method).

parameters.name: indexName
parameters.description:
This should be specified for documents that will be indexed by
            ElasticSearch and must be lower-case (ElasticSearch requires all lower-case). Because of changes in ElasticSearch 5
            this is no longer the actual index name and is instead an alias for each index from a DataDocument.

parameters.name: manualDataServiceName
parameters.description:
Name of a service to call to get additional
            data to include in the document. This service should implement the
            org.moqui.EntityServices.add#ManualDocumentData interface.

parameters.name: manualMappingServiceName
parameters.description:
Name of a service to call to alter the generated
            elasticsearch mapping for the data document. This service should implement the
            org.moqui.EntityServices.transform#DocumentMapping interface.

parameters.name: dataDocumentId
parameters.description:
For ElasticSearch compatibility, and as a general good
            practice for use as a dynamic view-entity, must follow entity name convention of camel case and starting with a capital letter.

parameters.name: dataDocumentId
parameters.description:
For ElasticSearch compatibility, and as a general good
            practice for use as a dynamic view-entity, must follow entity name convention of camel case and starting with a capital letter.

post.description: Index all documents associated with the feed within the date range. Recommend calling through the IndexDataFeedDocuments service job.

The queryString format is the ElasticSearch supported one, based on the Lucene query strings which are documented here:

            https://www.elastic.co/guide/en/elasticsearch/reference/current/query-dsl-query-string-query.html

            Sort options are described here:

            https://www.elastic.co/guide/en/elasticsearch/reference/current/search-request-body.html#request-body-search-sort

parameters.name: allowedToDomains
parameters.description: Comma separated list of domain names to allow (like: moqui.org, dejc.com), matched by ends with; if no value specified all domains allowed

post.description:
Poll an email server (IMAP or POP3) to receive messages. Each new message is processed using
            the Email-ECA rules. Messages are flagged as seen (if supported). Messages are deleted if the storeDelete
            flag is set on the moqui.basic.email.EmailServer record.

            This is meant to be called as a scheduled service, run as often as you want to poll for new messages on a
            particular server (configured in the corresponding moqui.basic.email.EmailServer record).

            Add a job in the quartz_data.xml file for this service to run this scheduled.

post.description: Create a moqui.basic.print.PrintJob record and send it to the specified NetworkPrinter

get.description:
Gets known local job details (from PrintJob record) job details/attributes from the print server,
            updating PrintJob record for status and just returning the rest.
Get printers from print server and create a moqui.basic.print.NetworkPrinter record for each.

parameters.name: pageIndex
parameters.description: Page number to return, starting with zero

parameters.name: pageSize
parameters.description: Number of records per page (default 100)

parameters.name: orderByField
parameters.description: Field name to order by (or comma separated names)

parameters.name: pageNoLimit
parameters.description: If true don't limit page size (no pagination)

parameters.name: dependentLevels
parameters.description: Levels of dependent child records to include

parameters.name: recordThreshold
parameters.description:
Keep retrieving time splits until the number of
            records is greater then this threshold.

parameters.name: delayBufferMillis
parameters.description:
Newer retrieve records newer than this many
            milliseconds in the past (leave a delay buffer for transactions in progress).

parameters.name: targetPath
parameters.description: For sending to via file the path and filename to use, or path/filename pattern using Groovy string expand

parameters.name: forPull
parameters.description:
If Y this record tracks data pulled from a remote
            system, otherwise it tracks data pushed from this system.

parameters.name: pageIndex
parameters.description: Page number to return, starting with zero

parameters.name: pageSize
parameters.description: Number of records per page (default 100)

parameters.name: orderByField
parameters.description: Field name to order by (or comma separated names)

parameters.name: pageNoLimit
parameters.description: If true don't limit page size (no pagination)

parameters.name: dependentLevels
parameters.description: Levels of dependent child records to include

parameters.name: systemMessageRemoteId
parameters.description:
Reference to the SystemMessageRemote record for the remote system
            this message came from for incoming messages or should be sent to for outgoing messages.

parameters.name: initDate
parameters.description: For incoming the received date, for outgoing the produced date

parameters.name: processedDate
parameters.description: For incoming the consumed date, for outgoing the sent date

parameters.name: parentMessageId
parameters.description: If a received message is split this is the original message

parameters.name: ackMessageId
parameters.description: The message received or sent to acknowledge this message

parameters.name: remoteMessageId
parameters.description:
For messages to/from another Moqui system, the
            systemMessageId on the remote system; may also be used for other system level message IDs (as opposed to
            messageId which is for the ID in the envelope of the message)

parameters.name: senderId
parameters.description:
ID of the sender (for OAGIS may be broken down into
            logicalId, component, task, referenceId; for EDI X12 this is ISA06)

parameters.name: receiverId
parameters.description:
ID of the receiver (for OAGIS may also be broken down;
            for EDI X12 this is ISA08)

parameters.name: messageId
parameters.description:
ID of the message; this may be globally unique (like
            the OAGIS BODID, a GUID) or only unique relative to the senderId and the receiverId (like EDI X12 ISA13 in
            the context of ISA06, ISA08), and may only be unique within a certain time period (ID may be reused since in
            EDI X12 limited to 9 digits)

parameters.name: messageDate
parameters.description:
Date/time from message (for EDI X12 this is GS04 (date)
            and GS05 (time))

parameters.name: docType
parameters.description: For OAGIS the BSR Noun; For X12 GS01 (functional ID code)

parameters.name: docSubType
parameters.description: For OAGIS the BSR Verb; For X12 ST01 (tx set ID code)

parameters.name: docControl
parameters.description:
Control number of the message when applicable (such as
            GS06 in EDI X12 messages)

parameters.name: docSubControl
parameters.description:
Sub-Control number of the message when applicable (such as
            ST02 in EDI X12 messages)

parameters.name: docVersion
parameters.description:
The document version (for OAGIS BSR Revision, for X12
            GS08 (version/revision))

parameters.name: triggerVisitId
parameters.description:
Active visit when SystemMessage triggered (mainly produced) to track
            the user who did so; independent of the message transport which could have separate remote system and other Visit-like data.

post.description:
Calls the send service (SystemMessageType.sendServiceName). Sets the SystemMessage status to
            SmsgSending while sending, then to SmsgSent if successful or back to original status if not. If the initial
            status is not SmsgProduced or SmsgError returns an error (generally means message already sent). If you
            want to resend a message that is in a later status, first change the status to SmsgProduced.

Calls the consume service (SystemMessageType.consumeServiceName). Sets the SystemMessage status to
            SmsgConsuming while consuming, then to SmsgConsumed if successful or back to original status if not. If the initial
            status is not SmsgReceived or SmsgError returns an error (generally means message already consumed). If you
            want to resend a message that is in a later status, first change the status to SmsgReceived.

            This uses a transaction timeout of 1800 seconds (30 minutes) as the default for the service and as the default for the
            consume service configured on the SystemMessageType. For incoming messages that require even more processing
            time it is best to break up the processing in a separate ServiceJob or other async service calls.

Queue an outgoing message. Creates a SystemMessage record for the outgoing message in the
            Produced status. If sendNow=true (default) will attempt to send it immediately, synchronously or asynchronously),
            otherwise the message will be picked up the next time the send#ProducedSystemMessages service runs.

Call the service to produce an async acknowledgement message (SystemMessageType.produceAckServiceName).
            If sendNow=true (default) will attempt to send it immediately (though asynchronously),
            otherwise the message will be picked up the next time the send#ProducedSystemMessages service runs.

Meant to be run scheduled, this service tries to send outgoing (isOutgoing=Y) messages in the
            SmsgProduced status. After retryLimit attempts will change the status to SmsgError.

Call to receive a message (often through a remote interface). If there is a
            SystemMessageType.receiveServiceName calls that service to save the message, otherwise creates a
            SystemMessage record for the incoming message (in the Received status). Either way after saving
            asynchronously calls the consume service based on the message type.

Consume incoming (isOutgoing=N) SystemMessage records not already consumed (in the SmsgReceived
            status). Messages in this state will normally have had an error in consuming. After retryLimit attempts
            will change the status to SmsgError.

parameters.name: pageIndex
parameters.description: Page number to return, starting with zero

parameters.name: pageSize
parameters.description: Number of records per page (default 100)

parameters.name: orderByField
parameters.description: Field name to order by (or comma separated names)

parameters.name: pageNoLimit
parameters.description: If true don't limit page size (no pagination)

parameters.name: dependentLevels
parameters.description: Levels of dependent child records to include

parameters.name: username
parameters.description: The username used along with the password to login

parameters.name: userFullName
parameters.description: User's first, middle, last, etc name

parameters.name: currentPassword
parameters.description: NOTE: not an encrypted field because one way hash encryption used for it

parameters.name: resetPassword
parameters.description: Set to random password for password reset, can be used only to update password

parameters.name: passwordBase64
parameters.description: Set to Y is currentPassword Base64 encoded, defaults to Hex encoded

parameters.name: publicKey
parameters.description: RSA public key for key based authentication

parameters.name: hasLoggedOut
parameters.description:
Set to Y when user logs out and to N when user logs in.
            If user is session authenticated on request and this is Y then treat as if user not authenticated.

parameters.name: terminateDate
parameters.description: If set then user may not login after this date, and no notifications will be sent after this date.

parameters.name: emailAddress
parameters.description: The email address to use for forgot password emails and other system messages.

parameters.name: ipAllowed
parameters.description:
If specified only allow login from matching IP4 address. Comma separated patterns where each pattern has 4
                dot separated segments each segment may be number, '*' for wildcard, or '-' separate number range (like '0-31').

post.description:
Set a user's password. The userId must match the current user and the oldPassword must match the
            user's currentPassword or special permission is required, or user has already pre-authenticated and specified an authz code.

parameters.name: userId
parameters.description: Defaults to the current userId in the ExecutionContext.

get.description: Get the published version of a wiki page by its space and path. If there is no published version behaves as if no page was found.

parameters.name: pagePathList
parameters.description: Alternative to pagePath when caller has a list of path elements (instead of forward slash separated string)

parameters.name: versionName
parameters.description:
Meant for testing, get this version instead of published.
                To eliminate exposure of non-published versions explicitly set this to null.

documentType.description: The ElasticSearch document type. For DataDocument based docs this is the dataDocumentId.

nestedQueryMap.description:
For explicit field constraints and such; key is path and
                may be null for a general query; value is a query JSON String (parsed to Map) or a query Map object

documentListCount.description: The total count of hits, not just the limited number returned.

toAddresses.description: Comma separated list of to email addresses

ccAddresses.description: Comma separated list of CC email addresses

bccAddresses.description: Comma separated list of BCC email addresses

messageId.description: From the Message-ID email header field.

emailMessageId.description: If createEmailMessage=true the ID of the EmailMessage record.

inputStream.description:
The document may be passed in this
                parameter as an InputStream or in the serialBlob field as a wrapped byte[].

serialBlob.description: Use SerialBlob as a wrapper for byte[].

screenParametersStr.description: Groovy expression that evaluates to a Map

includeFilterList.description: List of Maps to be ORed together
List of Maps to be ORed together

systemMessageId.description:
Sequenced if null, may be passed in (sequenced value
                determined in advance) because sometimes this is needed as a reference ID inside a message.

systemMessageRemoteId.description:
Required if the send service
                (SystemMessageType.sendServiceName) requires it. The send#SystemMessageJsonRpc service does require it.
If not specified comes from SystemMessage.systemMessageRemoteId

userId.description: Defaults to the current userId in the ExecutionContext.

username.description: May be used instead of userId to identify user.

oldPassword.description: Ignored if user has password admin permissions.

code.description: Second factor authentication, required if second factor required for user (via group or authc factors configured)

username.description: May be used instead of userId to identify user.
